articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

keyEvents.text: Goal! VfB Stuttgart 1, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 0. Ermedin Demirovic (VfB Stuttgart) right footed shot from very close range to the bottom right corner.
Atakan Karazor (VfB Stuttgart) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Second Half begins VfB Stuttgart 1, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 0.
Goal! VfB Stuttgart 2, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 0. Nick Woltemade (VfB Stuttgart) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Enzo Millot following a fast break.
Finn Jeltsch (VfB Stuttgart) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! VfB Stuttgart 2, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 1. Jeremie Frimpong (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner following a set piece situation.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Amine Adli replaces Aleix García.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Victor Boniface replaces Álex Grimaldo.
Own Goal by Granit Xhaka, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. VfB Stuttgart 3, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 1.
Nick Woltemade (VfB Stuttgart) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! VfB Stuttgart 3, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2. Piero Hincapié (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the top left corner following a corner.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Deniz Undav replaces Enzo Millot.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Josha Vagnoman replaces Jamie Leweling.
Robert Andrich (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Arthur replaces Robert Andrich.
Piero Hincapié (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. El Bilal Touré replaces Ermedin Demirovic.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Jeff Chabot replaces Ameen Al Dakhil.
Own Goal by Angelo Stiller, VfB Stuttgart. VfB Stuttgart 3, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 3.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Chris Führich replaces Maximilian Mittelstädt.
Goal! VfB Stuttgart 3, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 4. Patrik Schick (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) header from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Jeremie Frimpong with a cross.
Second Half ends, VfB Stuttgart 3, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 4.

article.story:
Reigning champions Bayer Leverkusen snatched a 4-3 win at VfB Stuttgart with two late goals on Sunday to snap a three-game losing run in all competitions and cut Bayern Munich's lead atop the Bundesliga to six points.

Patrik Schick struck in stoppage time to give the visitors the points after they had drawn level minutes earlier courtesy of Stuttgart midfielder Angelo Stiller's 88th-minute own goal.

Five days after their Champions League Round of 16 exit to Bayern following defeats in both legs, Leverkusen bounced back from last week's 2-0 home defeat by Werder Bremen.

The result lifted Leverkusen to 56 points in second place, cutting Bayern's lead to six after their 1-1 draw at Union Berlin on Saturday as the teams went into the international break.

"The late goal was a magic moment for us and the celebrations were very emotional," Leverkusen coach Xabi Alonso said. "Compliments to my players who never gave up. We had the will until the very end to get those three points.

"Stuttgart played a very strong game. This win is very good for us after the defeat this week in the Champions League."

Leverkusen are also through to the German Cup last four where they face third-tier Arminia Bielefeld next month.

Ermedin Demirovic put Stuttgart ahead in the 15th minute before the unmarked Nick Woltemade slotted home to complete a quick break and make it 2-0 three minutes after halftime.

Leverkusen's Jeremie Frimpong cut the deficit in the 56th after Stuttgart repeatedly failed to clear the ball.

However, the hosts restored their two-goal lead after 62 minutes when goalkeeper Lukas Hradecky saved a Demirovic shot but the ball bounced off Granit Xhaka for an own goal.

Six minutes later, it was Leverkusen's turn to score with Piero Hincapie drilling in a shot to give them renewed hope.

Amine Adli came agonizingly close to an equalizer in the 82nd, but his shot just missed before Stiller scored an own goal.

Schick completed Leverkusen's pulsating late comeback in stoppage time to keep them firmly in the title race.
